title: The Constitution of India: Modern Annotated Edition
author: B.R. Ambedkar
rationale: The Constitution of India is the foundational legal text of the world's largest democracy and constitutional republic. As root Book #1 for the indian-law leaf, it provides the indispensable starting point for any study of Indian law, governance, or constitutional democracy. Written under Ambedkar's chairmanship of the Drafting Committee over nearly three years of Constituent Assembly deliberation, it is the world's longest written national constitution and one of the most ambitious attempts to embed social revolution as a constitutional commitment. The OUP India annotated edition provides modern editorial context essential for contemporary readers.
verification_source: Oxford University Press India catalog; Open Library ISBN 9780199455503; ISBN registered OUP India 2017 annotated edition
